questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A charge moving with a constant velocity does not produce any magnetic field.
questions.reason: Reason (R): A magnetic field is produced by a moving charge when there is a change in its velocity or direction.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because when a charge is moving with a constant velocity, no magnetic field is produced. Reason R explains why this is true, as it states that a magnetic field is produced only when there is a change in velocity or direction.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The magnetic field produced by a current-carrying straight wire is uniform.

opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because Assertion A is false, as the magnetic field produced by a current-carrying straight wire is not uniform, but Reason R is true.
